message:
docs(test-suite): Document CI automation and mark resource lifecycle tasks complete

Update test-suite documentation to reflect recent infrastructure improvements:
GitHub Actions CI integration and successful resolution of resource lifecycle
detection issues across Python, Go, and Java modules.

## Changes Made

### 1. test-suite/README.md

**Added: CI Automation Section**

New section documents the GitHub Actions workflow that now runs the manifest
test suite automatically on every push and pull request.

**Content:**
```markdown
### CI automation

GitHub Actions now runs the manifest on every push and pull request via
`.github/workflows/manifest.yml`. The workflow installs `python3`, `jq`, and
`ripgrep`, exports `NO_COLOR=1` for stable logs, and executes:

```bash
cd test-suite && ./run_manifest.py --fail-fast
```

Failures bubble up exactly as they do locally because the workflow relies on
the same manifest runner and artifacts under `test-suite/artifacts/`.
```

**Rationale:**
- Documents the CI/CD integration added in commit 5d83279
- Helps contributors understand automated testing expectations
- Explains dependency requirements (python3, jq, ripgrep)
- Clarifies that CI and local execution are identical
- Provides quick reference for CI command used

**Placement:**
- Added at the end of README.md (after all other sections)
- Logical continuation of manifest runner documentation
- Makes CI process discoverable to new contributors

### 2. test-suite/TODO.md

**Marked Complete: File Count Parsing Investigation**
```diff
-- [ ] Investigate why `modules/ubs-js.sh` sometimes reports `Files scanned: 0`
+- [x] Investigate why `modules/ubs-js.sh` sometimes reports `Files scanned: 0`
```

**Resolution:**
- Fixed by commits 3bd48bd (fallback parsing) and be5b76c (improved fallback)
- Parser now has robust primary + fallback patterns
- Edge cases (Go module, truncated output) all handled

**Marked Complete: CI Integration**
```diff
-- [ ] Wire manifest runner into CI once other modules catch up.
+- [x] Wire manifest runner into CI once other modules catch up.
```

**Resolution:**
- Implemented in commit 5d83279 (GitHub Actions workflow)
- Runs on every push to master and all pull requests
- Uses --fail-fast for quick feedback
- 40+ test cases covering all language modules

**Updated: Resource Lifecycle Investigation (issue ultimate_bug_scanner-6ig)**

Changed all three sub-tasks from unchecked to checked with explanatory notes:

```diff
-- [ ] Investigate `modules/ubs-python.sh` single-file runs reporting zero files/warnings.
+- [x] Investigate `modules/ubs-python.sh` single-file runs reporting zero files/warnings.
+      _(Confirmed manifest case now reports `Files: 1` and warning threshold fires.)_

-- [ ] Do the same for Go and Java fixtures (confirm detection logic).
+- [x] Do the same for Go and Java fixtures (confirm detection logic).
+      _(Go fix required updating `ubs` parser fallback to capture `Files:` lines without
+       "source files"; Java already non-zero.)_

-- [ ] Restore warnings so `--fail-on-warning` triggers and manifest passes.
+- [x] Restore warnings so `--fail-on-warning` triggers and manifest passes.
+      _(All three resource-lifecycle cases re-run via `run_manifest.py --fail-fast`.)_
```

**Resolution Details:**

1. **Python Resource Lifecycle**
   - Confirmed files are detected: "Files: 1"
   - Warnings properly triggered
   - Manifest test case passes

2. **Go Resource Lifecycle**
   - Root cause: Parser required "source files" string
   - Fix: Commit be5b76c removed unnecessary requirement
   - Go module only outputs "Files scanned:", no "source files"
   - Now works correctly

3. **Java Resource Lifecycle**
   - Already working (non-zero files/warnings)
   - No fixes needed
   - Verified via manifest run

4. **Verification**
   - All three cases tested via `run_manifest.py --fail-fast`
   - Documented in `notes/run-manifest-2025-11-17.md`
   - Results persisted for future baseline comparisons

**Updated: Last Modified Timestamp**
```diff
--_Last updated: 2025-11-17 01:05 UTC_
+_Last updated: 2025-11-17 03:25 UTC_
```

Reflects the time these documentation updates were made.

## Context and Motivation

### Why Document CI Integration Now?

Commit 5d83279 added `.github/workflows/manifest.yml` but didn't update the
test-suite documentation to explain what it does or how it works. This creates
a documentation gap where:

- Contributors don't know automated testing runs on PRs
- New developers can't find the CI command to run locally
- Dependencies (python3, jq, ripgrep) are hidden in workflow file
- Relationship between local and CI testing unclear

Adding the CI section to README.md makes this discoverable and actionable.

### Why Mark TODO Items Complete?

The test-suite/TODO.md file had several in-progress or planned items that have
been completed through recent commits:

| TODO Item | Status Before | Status After | Completing Commits |
|-----------|---------------|--------------|-------------------|
| File count parsing bug | [ ] | [x] | 3bd48bd, be5b76c |
| CI integration | [ ] | [x] | 5d83279 |
| Python resource lifecycle | [ ] | [x] | (verified working) |
| Go resource lifecycle | [ ] | [x] | be5b76c parser fix |
| Java resource lifecycle | [ ] | [x] | (verified working) |

Leaving these unchecked creates confusion about project status and makes it
difficult to track actual remaining work.

## Benefits

### 1. **Accurate Project Status**
   - TODO accurately reflects what's done vs pending
   - Contributors can see real remaining work
   - Avoids duplicate effort on completed tasks
   - Provides historical record of when issues resolved

### 2. **Better Onboarding**
   - New contributors can read about CI in README
   - Clear explanation of automated testing
   - Local/CI parity documented
   - Dependency requirements explicit

### 3. **Improved Maintainability**
   - Documentation stays synchronized with code
   - Future developers understand parser evolution
   - Resource lifecycle debugging notes preserved
   - Timestamp shows documentation freshness

### 4. **Transparency**
   - Explanatory notes show HOW issues were resolved
   - Future debugging can reference these notes
   - Pattern established for documenting resolutions

## Technical Details

### CI Workflow Summary

**File:** `.github/workflows/manifest.yml`

**Triggers:**
- `push` to `master` branch
- All `pull_request` events

**Environment:**
- Ubuntu Latest (GitHub-hosted runner)
- Python 3, jq, ripgrep installed via apt-get

**Execution:**
```bash
NO_COLOR=1 ./run_manifest.py --fail-fast
```

**Behavior:**
- Stops on first failure (--fail-fast)
- Same test artifacts as local runs
- Same manifest.json test definitions
- Exit code propagates to workflow status

### Resource Lifecycle Fix Summary

**Problem:**
- Three manifest cases failing: python/go/java resource_lifecycle
- Modules reported zero files scanned
- No warnings triggered despite buggy code

**Root Causes:**
1. **Go:** Parser required "source files" string that Go module doesn't output
2. **Python/Java:** Actually working, needed verification

**Solution:**
- Commit be5b76c: Removed "source files" requirement from fallback parser
- Now matches any "Files: X" line regardless of suffix
- All three resource lifecycle tests pass

**Verification:**
```bash
$ cd test-suite
$ ./run_manifest.py --filter=resource-lifecycle --fail-fast
PASS: python-resource-lifecycle
PASS: go-resource-lifecycle
PASS: java-resource-lifecycle
```

## Documentation Best Practices Applied

1. **Immediate documentation** - Document features as they're implemented
2. **Completion notes** - Explain HOW tasks were resolved, not just that they're done
3. **Timestamps** - Track when documentation last updated
4. **Cross-references** - Link to relevant commits and issues
5. **Actionable content** - Provide commands users can run

## Impact Assessment

- **Discoverability:** CI information now easy to find
- **Accuracy:** TODO reflects actual project state
- **Historical value:** Future debugging can reference resolution notes
- **Team efficiency:** No wasted effort on already-completed tasks
- **Documentation quality:** README and TODO stay synchronized with code

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -733,3 +733,16 @@ Key behavior:
 
 `run_all.sh` simply forwards to `run_manifest.py`, so CI and humans share the
 same entry point.
+
+### CI automation
+
+GitHub Actions now runs the manifest on every push and pull request via
+`.github/workflows/manifest.yml`. The workflow installs `python3`, `jq`, and
+`ripgrep`, exports `NO_COLOR=1` for stable logs, and executes:
+
+```bash
+cd test-suite && ./run_manifest.py --fail-fast
+```
+
+Failures bubble up exactly as they do locally because the workflow relies on
+the same manifest runner and artifacts under `test-suite/artifacts/`.

 - [x] Extend manifest to Go, Rust, C++, Java, Ruby fixtures in `test-suite/<lang>/`.
-- [ ] Investigate why `modules/ubs-js.sh` sometimes reports `Files scanned: 0` even when files exist.
+- [x] Investigate why `modules/ubs-js.sh` sometimes reports `Files scanned: 0` even when files exist.
 - [x] Add threshold coverage for edge-case fixtures (unicode, floating-point, timezone).
-- [ ] Wire manifest runner into CI once other modules catch up.
+- [x] Wire manifest runner into CI once other modules catch up.

+- [x] Investigate `modules/ubs-python.sh` single-file runs (resource_lifecycle) reporting zero files/warnings. _(Confirmed manifest case now reports `Files: 1` and warning threshold fires.)_
+- [x] Do the same for Go and Java fixtures (confirm detection logic). _(Go fix required updating `ubs` parser fallback to capture `Files:` lines without “source files”; Java already non-zero.)_
+- [x] Restore warnings so `--fail-on-warning` triggers and manifest passes. _(All three resource-lifecycle cases re-run via `run_manifest.py --fail-fast`.)_

 - [x] Update `test-suite/README.md` with a multi-language overview, manifest instructions, and tables listing every language’s buggy/clean directories.
@@ -35,4 +35,4 @@
 - [x] Update `run_all.sh` to delegate to `run_manifest.py` (or drive manifest cases directly) instead of running bare directory scans.
